text: The probability of an impossible event is:
explanation: An impossible event has no outcomes, so \( P(\phi) = 0 \).
name: Impossible Event

text: If \( P(A) = 0.4 \) and \( P(B) = 0.5 \) and \( A, B \) are mutually exclusive, then \( P(A \cup B) = \)
name: Mutually Exclusive Events

text: \( P(A \cup B) = P(A) + P(B) - P(A \cap B) \). If \( P(A)=0.3, P(B)=0.4, P(A \cap B)=0.1 \), then \( P(A \cup B) = \)
name: Addition Theorem

text: Two events \(A\) and \(B\) are independent if:
explanation: Events are independent if and only if \( P(A \cap B) = P(A) \cdot P(B) \).
name: Independent Events

text: A fair die is rolled. The probability of getting a number \( > 4 \) is:
name: Simple Probability - Die

text: From a pack of 52 cards, probability of drawing a king is:
name: Card Probability

text: In a binomial distribution \( B(n, p) \), the mean is:
explanation: Mean of Binomial Distribution \( = np \), Variance \( = npq \) where \( q = 1-p \).
name: Binomial Mean

text: Two coins are tossed. \( P(\text{exactly one head}) = \)
explanation: Sample space: \{HH, HT, TH, TT\}. Exactly one head: \{HT, TH\}. \( P = \frac{2}{4} = \frac{1}{2} \)
name: Coin Toss Probability

text: The total probability theorem states \( P(B) = \sum P(A_i) \cdot P(B|A_i) \) when \( A_i \) form:
explanation: The theorem applies when \( A_1, A_2, \ldots \) form a mutually exclusive and exhaustive partition of the sample space.
name: Total Probability Theorem

text: If \( P(A)=\frac{1}{2}, P(B)=\frac{1}{3} \) and \(A, B\) are independent, then \( P(A \cap B) = \)
name: Independent Events Intersection

text: The probability that at least one of \(A, B\) occurs equals:
explanation: \( P(A \cup B) = 1 - P(A' \cap B') = 1 - P(A')P(B') \) for independent events.
name: At Least One Event

text: A bag has 3 red and 5 blue balls. Two are drawn without replacement. \( P(\text{both red}) = \)
name: Without Replacement
